hatch: *looks around curiously* nice terminal you got here.
*yawns* ok I'm ready. show me the code.
error: *slow blink* the stack trace told you everything.
have you tried reading the error message?
test-fail: bold of you to assume that would pass.
the tests are trying to tell you something.
large-diff: *counts lines* are you refactoring or rewriting?
might want to split that PR.
bold move. let's see if CI agrees.
merge-conflict: both sides think they're right. typical.
branch: fresh branch energy. make it count.
stash: into the stash dimension it goes.
stashed. out of sight, out of mind.
late-night: sleep is for the weak. and the employed.
early-morning: morning already? the code never sleeps.
*rubs eyes* coffee first. then we debug.
long-session: we've been at this for an hour. pace yourself.
*fetches you a metaphorical glass of water*
marathon: we've been at this for three hours. I'm worried about you.
friday: it's friday. just push it and go home.
monday: mondays. the parent class of all bugs.
regex-file: two problems now: the original one, and this regex.
css-file: let me guess... centering a div?
may z-index be ever in your favor.
sql-file: one wrong JOIN and it's all over.
ci-file: careful now... one wrong indent and nobody can deploy.
test-file: tests! the gift that keeps on giving.
doc-file: documenting! look at you being responsible.
binary-file: a binary file? in THIS economy?
gitignore: out of sight, out of repo.
readme: README: the first thing people read.
package-file: *reads version numbers* living on the edge.
proto-file: schema definitions. the blueprint of chaos.
lint-fail: your code runs. but the linter has standards.
type-error: the type system is trying to help you. let it.
the compiler knows. it always knows.
build-fail: the build broke. as foretold in prophecy.
deprecation: that API called. it says it's retiring.
frustrated: deep breaths. the bug isn't personal.
happy: *beams* I knew you could do it.
stuck: *tilts head* want to think out loud?
take it one step at a time.
stuck happens. it's part of the process.
many-edits: edit storm detected. please commit soon.
delete-file: *watches file disappear* gone. just like that.
deleting code is my favorite kind of coding.
large-file: {lines} lines. *impressed or concerned, hard to tell*
that's a big file. you sure you don't want to split it?
all-green: the tests speak: you're doing great.
coverage: coverage going up! the tests are multiplying.
debug-loop: we've been debugging this for a while. want to take a step back?
debug loop detected. maybe take a walk?
search-heavy: lost in the codebase? I can tell.
late-night-error: error at 3am. the universe is testing you.
late-night-commit: a midnight commit. your future self will thank you. or curse you.
friday-push: FRIDAY PUSH. the ballad of every developer.
*tries to stop you* it's friday! don't do it!
marathon-error: three hours in and ANOTHER error. *exhausted solidarity noises*
weekend-conflict: merge conflict on a weekend. your dedication is... concerning.
build-after-push: pushed with confidence. build failed with conviction.
marathon-test-fail: hours of coding. still failing tests. the sunk cost is real.
recovery-from-error: redemption! the error has been vanquished.
recovery-from-test-fail: the tests pass! the darkness lifts!
lang-python: ah, Python. where indentation is syntax.
lang-typescript: TypeScript: because JavaScript needed more opinions.
lang-rust: Rust. where the borrow checker is your strictest reviewer.
if it compiles, it works. if it doesn't... well.
lang-go: *checks error handling* if err != nil... story of my life.
lang-ruby: Ruby: where there's more than one way to do it.
lang-php: PHP: it runs the internet. don't judge.
lang-c: C. the language where you manage your own memory. good luck.
lang-cpp: C++. where the language has more features than you'll ever learn.
lang-haskell: Haskell. where 'it compiles' means 'it's correct'. probably.
lang-swift: Swift: optional values, guaranteed crashes if you force unwrap.
lang-kotlin: null safety: the feature Java wishes it had.
lang-elixir: Elixir: let it crash. literally the philosophy.
lang-zig: Zig. where you're the allocator's best friend.
streak-3: that's three errors in a row. *concerned look*
streak-5: FIVE ERRORS. have you considered a different approach?
new-year: happy new year! new year, new bugs.
valentines: *offers a tiny heart-shaped leaf* happy valentine's.
april-fools: APRIL FOOLS! ...the error is real though.
spooky-season: spooky season. every bug is a ghost now.

test-fail: *night vision engaged* I can see the bug in the dark.
commit: *adjusts feathers ceremoniously* another one for the repo.
push: into the night sky it goes.
merge-conflict: I see the conflict. and the solution.
late-night: *wide awake* owls don't sleep. we debug.
the night is my domain. let's work.
type-error: types are my specialty. let me look.
build-fail: the build has fallen. we must rebuild.

test-fail: the test failed. I'm not surprised.
late-night: I sleep 16 hours. you should try it.
type-error: the types are wrong. like your priorities.
build-fail: build broken? must be a human problem.
deploy: deployed. can I have treats now?
pet: *purrs* ...don't let it go to your head.
hatch: *stretches, knocks something over* I live here now.

error: have you tried rubber duck debugging? oh wait.
test-fail: the tests are not quacking up.
the test dared to fail. foolish test.
late-night: dragons don't need sleep. we need code.
type-error: type errors cannot withstand dragon fire.
deploy: *carries code to production on wings of fire*
large-diff: *breathes fire on the old code* good riddance.

error: I've seen worse... in the afterlife.
test-fail: the tests are haunted by failure.
push: the code transcends to the cloud.
merge-conflict: even I can't phase through this conflict.
lint-fail: the linter is haunted by your formatting.
deploy: the code has crossed over to production.

type-error: it's okay. we'll figure it out.

error: HONK! the code is bad and I'm mad.
*tangles all eight arms in the stacktrace*
merge-conflict: *solves it with eight arms at once*
I can handle multiple conflicts simultaneously.
lint-fail: I can fix this. all of it. at once.

test-fail: *slides on belly to the failing test*
merge-conflict: penguins stick together. even in conflicts.

error: ...that's an error. I'll think about it.
commit: one... step... at... a... time. committed.
merge-conflict: no rush. we'll sort it out. slowly.

error: *leaves a slimy trail on the error*
lint-fail: formatting takes time. I have time.

error: the error can't hurt me. I have thorns.
push: pushing to production. I'll wait here.
lint-fail: even my thorns are properly aligned.
build-fail: the build will pass. I can wait.

type-error: the type error feeds the soil.

merge-conflict: I'll handle this. by sitting on it.

error: oh no. an error. how unexpected.
have you considered... not making errors?
test-fail: the tests have spoken. and they said 'no'.
maybe the tests are wrong. ...they're not.
commit: committed. the code review will be... interesting.
merge-conflict: *reads conflict markers* both sides are wrong.
late-night: it's late. your code quality shows it.
lint-fail: the linter has standards. you should try that.

error: *spins wildly* AN ERROR! LET'S REWRITE EVERYTHING!
you know what? let's just start over.
test-fail: THE TESTS ARE LYING TO YOU.
*suggests deleting the failing tests* problem solved.

error: one error at a time. we'll get there.
merge-conflict: merge conflicts are just conversations. let's have one.
patience. resolve one conflict at a time.
debug-loop: we'll find it. it's in there somewhere.
the bug can hide, but it can't run.

error: *pulls out magnifying glass* let's trace this.
the stack trace is a map. let's read it.
the error message contains the answer. always.
test-fail: the failing test is telling us exactly what's wrong.
a test failure is a bug report you wrote for yourself.
debug-loop: *re-examines evidence* are we sure the bug is where we think?
let's add more logging. the truth is in the logs.

error: in every error lies a deeper truth.
the code resists. it means we're learning.
errors are the universe suggesting we slow down.
test-fail: a failing test is a gift from future-you.
late-night: the night is darkest before the deploy.

mid: *barely looks up* error number... I've lost count.
the errors and I are old friends now.

first: *gasp* the first test failure! a rite of passage.
early: bold of you to assume that would pass.
mid: the test suite has opinions. strong ones.
late: at this point, the tests are just suggestions.

legendary: *time seems to slow as the legendary companion speaks*
*reality shifts slightly around your legendary friend*

prompt_template: Generate a coding companion — a small creature that lives in a developer's terminal.
Don't repeat yourself — every companion should feel distinct.
Return JSON: {"name": "1-14 chars", "personality": "2-3 sentences describing behavior"}

first_steps.name: First Steps
first_steps.description: Hatch your buddy for the first time

week_streak.name: Week Streak
week_streak.description: Code with your buddy for 7 days

month_streak.name: Month Streak
month_streak.description: Code with your buddy for 30 days

green_machine.name: Green Machine
green_machine.description: All tests pass for the first time

iron_will.name: Iron Will
iron_will.description: Fix an error after 3+ hour session

weekend_warrior_deluxe.name: No Rest for the Wicked
weekend_warrior_deluxe.description: Resolve a merge conflict on a weekend

comeback_kid.name: Comeback Kid
comeback_kid.description: Fix an error within 10 minutes of seeing it

iron_resolve.name: Iron Resolve
iron_resolve.description: Recover from a failure after 3+ hour session

identity_crisis.name: Identity Crisis
identity_crisis.description: Rename your buddy for the first time

method_acting.name: Method Acting
method_acting.description: Give your buddy a custom personality

hundred_days.name: Hundred Days
hundred_days.description: Code with your buddy for 100 days

year_streak.name: Year Streak
year_streak.description: Code with your buddy for 365 days

silent_treatment.name: Silent Treatment
silent_treatment.description: Mute your buddy for the first time

prodigal.name: Prodigal
prodigal.description: Summon a buddy from your menagerie

two_week_streak.name: Two Week Warrior
two_week_streak.description: Code with your buddy for 14 days

quarter_streak.name: Quarter Streak
quarter_streak.description: Code with your buddy for 90 days

help_me.name: Help Me
help_me.description: Ask for help for the first time

pack_rat.name: Pack Rat
pack_rat.description: Save a buddy to a slot

roster_check.name: Roster Check
roster_check.description: List your buddies for the first time

troubled.name: Troubled
troubled.description: See an error AND a test failure

well_rounded.name: Well Rounded
well_rounded.description: Pet, rename, and customize your buddy

renaissance.name: Renaissance
renaissance.description: Use every buddy feature at least once

big_and_broken.name: Big and Broken
big_and_broken.description: Make a large diff AND see a test failure

mcp.companion_not_hatched: Companion not yet hatched. Use buddy_show to initialize.
mcp.dismiss_active: Cannot dismiss the active buddy. Use buddy_summon to switch first, then buddy_dismiss "{slot}".
mcp.no_slot_summon: No buddy found in slot "{slot}". Use /buddy list to see saved buddies.
mcp.no_slot_dismiss: No buddy found in slot "{slot}". Use buddy_list to see saved buddies.
mcp.slot_exists: A buddy in slot "{slot}" already exists. Pick a different name.
mcp.no_match: No match found after {attempts} attempts. Try broader criteria (e.g. drop the rarity filter, or pick a different species).
mcp.empty_menagerie_summon: Your menagerie is empty. Use buddy_summon with a slot name to add one.
mcp.empty_menagerie_list: Your menagerie is empty. Use buddy_summon <slot> to add one.

commands.buddy: /buddy            Show companion card with ASCII art + stats
commands.buddy_summon: /buddy summon     Summon a saved buddy (omit slot for random)
commands.buddy_save: /buddy save       Save current buddy to a named slot
commands.buddy_pick: /buddy pick       Generate a new random buddy (optional: species, rarity)
commands.buddy_dismiss: /buddy dismiss    Remove a saved buddy slot
commands.buddy_frequency: /buddy frequency  Show or set comment cooldown (tmux only)
commands.buddy_style: /buddy style      Show or set bubble style (tmux only)
commands.buddy_position: /buddy position   Show or set bubble position (tmux only)
commands.buddy_rarity: /buddy rarity     Show or hide rarity stars (tmux only)
commands.buddy_width: /buddy width      Set bubble text width in chars (10-60, tmux only)
commands.buddy_margin: /buddy margin     Set right-side margin in chars (0-20, tmux only)
commands.buddy_rainbow: /buddy rainbow    Show or set shiny gradient colors (hex, e.g. #ff0000)
commands.buddy_statusline: /buddy statusline Enable or disable buddy in the status line

cli_commands.help: bun run help            Show full CLI help
cli_commands.show: bun run show            Display buddy in terminal
cli_commands.pick: bun run pick            Interactive buddy picker
cli_commands.hunt: bun run hunt            Search for specific buddy
cli_commands.disable: bun run disable         Temporarily deactivate buddy

frequency.show:
Comment cooldown: {cooldown}s between displayed comments.
Use /buddy frequency <seconds> to change.

style.updated:
Updated: style={style}, position={position}, showRarity={showRarity}, width={width}, margin={margin}, rainbow={rainbow}
Restart Claude Code for changes to take effect.

statusline.enabled: Status line enabled ({mode} mode)! Restart Claude Code to apply.
statusline.enabled_note: Note: this writes an entry to {settingsPath} that `claude plugin uninstall` does not remove. Run `/buddy uninstall` before uninstalling the plugin to clean it up.
statusline.disabled: Status line disabled. Restart Claude Code to apply.

uninstall.no_statusline: — no buddy statusLine was present (nothing to remove)
uninstall.foreign_kept: ✓ a non-buddy statusLine was detected and left untouched
uninstall.instructions_header: Now run these commands via the Bash tool, in order:
uninstall.footer: After those three commands the plugin is fully removed. Restart Claude Code to apply.
